claim: ILO and UNICEF estimate 160 million children in child labor globally, 70 percent in agriculture, highest absolute numbers in Sub-Saharan Africa, with prevalence concentrated in low-income agricultural households.

source.title: Child Labour: Global estimates 2020, trends and the road forward

method: Reviewed ILO-UNICEF joint estimates, sectoral breakdown, and methodology.
limitations: Estimates are modeled from surveys of variable recency and comparability.
Sub-national breakdowns are not available from global estimates.

claim: A 2022 analysis found that linking household-survey child labor prevalence to crop-production maps enables supply-chain risk estimation, but survey coverage gaps in high-risk countries remain the primary constraint.

source.title: Linking child labor data to agricultural supply chains: methods and limitations

method: Reviewed linking methodology, crop-production mapping, and survey-gap analysis.
limitations: Survey-to-crop attribution is probabilistic, not verified at farm level.
Supply-chain traceability data is proprietary and incomplete.
